mnemonics: V-Tables are Virtual Vehicle Tables that help decide which function to drive to at runtime.
Remember: Virtual functions let the Right object (the actual derived class object) do the Right thing (call its overridden function).
Polymorphism = One Interface, Many Forms. Think of a 'PLAY' button - it works for Music, Video, and Podcasts.
keyPoints: A virtual function is a member function declared with the 'virtual' keyword in a base class and can be overridden in derived classes.
Polymorphism allows a base class pointer or reference to call the appropriate derived class method at runtime, based on the actual object type.
The mechanism is implemented using a virtual function table (vtable) for each class that contains virtual functions, which holds pointers to the correct function implementations.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to declare the destructor as virtual in a base class when you have derived classes, which can lead to resource leaks.
Attempting to declare a function as virtual in a derived class without it being virtual in the base class first (though override keyword in C++11 helps catch this).
Expecting virtual functions to work with class objects themselves (static binding) instead of through pointers or references (dynamic binding).
